children.heading: Overall negotiating objective
children.label: a Overall negotiating objective
children.text: The President shall seek in the renegotiations provided for in article XXIV(7) of the Agreement more open and equitable market access abroad, and the harmonization, reduction, or elimination of devices which distort trade or commerce related to Government procurement, with the overall goal of maximizing the economic benefit to the United States through maintaining and enlarging foreign markets for products of United States agriculture, industry, mining, and commerce, the development of fair and equitable market opportunities, and open and nondiscriminatory world trade. In carrying out the provisions of this subsection, the President shall consider the assessment made in the report required under section 2516(a) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Sector negotiating objectives
children.label: b Sector negotiating objectives
children.text: The President shall seek, consistent with the overall objective set forth in subsection (a) and to the maximum extent feasible, with respect to appropriate product sectors, competitive opportunities for the export of United States products to the developed countries of the world equivalent to the competitive opportunities afforded by the United States, taking into account all barriers to, and other distortions of, international trade affecting that sector.

children.heading: Independent verification objective
children.label: c Independent verification objective
children.text: The President shall seek to establish in the renegotiation provided for in article XXIV(7) of the Agreement a system for independent verification of information provided by parties to the Agreement to the Committee on Government Procurement pursuant to article XIX(5) of the Agreement.

children.heading: Report in the event of inadequate progress
children.label: 1 Report in the event of inadequate progress
children.text: If, during the renegotiations of the Agreement, the President at any time determines that the renegotiations are not progressing satisfactorily and are not likely to result, within twelve months of the commencement thereof, in an expansion of the Agreement to cover purchases by the entities of the governments of developed countries which are the principal purchasers of goods and equipment in appropriate product sectors, he shall so report to the congressional committees referred to in . Taking into account the objectives set forth in subsections (a) and (b) of this section and the factors required to be analyzed under , the President shall further report to such committees appropriate actions to seek reciprocity in such product sectors with such countries in the area of government procurement.section 2512(c)(1) of this titlesection 2512(c) of this title

children.heading: Legislative recommendations
children.label: 2 Legislative recommendations
children.text: Taking into account the factors required to be analyzed under , the President may recommend to the Congress legislation (with respect to entities of the Government which are not covered by the Agreement) which may prohibit such entities from purchasing products of such countries.section 2512(c) of this title

children.heading: Annual reports
children.label: 3 Annual reports
children.text: Each annual report of the President under section 163(a) of the Trade Act of 1974 [] made after shall report the actions, if any, the President deemed appropriate to establish reciprocity in appropriate product sectors with major industrial countries in the area of government procurement.19 U.S.C. 2213(a)July 26, 1979

children.heading: Extension of nondiscrimination and national treatment
children.label: e Extension of nondiscrimination and national treatment
children.text: Before exercising the waiver authority in for procurement not covered by the Agreement on the date it enters into force with respect to the United States, the President shall follow the consultation provisions of section 135 [] and chapter 6 of title I of the Trade Act of 1974 [ et seq.] for private sector and congressional consultations.section 2511 of this title19 U.S.C. 215519 U.S.C. 2211
